failure_modes.text: Teams can miss crucial anomalies when old patterns feel familiar enough to suppress the cue that this case is different.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Pattern Recognition Stops Updating.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build anomaly-detection habits: train pattern experts to also practice noticing what does not fit the expected pattern, not just what confirms it.

failure_modes.text: False pattern recognition can produce disastrously wrong solutions when the handy frame gets applied to a genuinely novel problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: False Pattern Recognition and Availability Heuristic
failure_modes.mitigation: Before applying a recognized pattern, check structural fit: list three features the current case shares with the template and one it does not, then judge whether the mismatch matters.

failure_modes.text: Experts can become hard to teach or understand when refined pattern libraries turn into abstraction that no longer matches novice needs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair expert pattern-matchers with structured novice explanations: require the expert to articulate the pattern in terms a newcomer can verify, exposing abstraction gaps.

premortem_questions.text: What subtle cue would tell us this case does not belong in the familiar pattern bucket we are reaching for first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when better pattern detection changes real-world performance.

premortem_questions.text: Which signal matters here, and which distractions are we still treating as if they were equally important?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when a domain rewards anomaly detection, signal filtering, or cue discrimination.

premortem_questions.text: Where has expert abstraction drifted so far from the novice view that the real lesson no longer transfers?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Experts, having refined their models through PL, find it difficult to remember what it was like not to know something.

heuristics.text: Train pattern recognition with stories, timelines, and decision walkthroughs that expose the cues experts actually use.
Use plain-English explanation and analogy to expose blind spots in the pattern model before it hardens.
Tune the message to the audience so the critical pattern becomes visible instead of staying trapped in expert abstraction.
heuristics.source_quote: Complex ideas ... must be intentionally retuned for the croc brain.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on anomaly detection, cue filtering, and anti-entrenchment rather than on broad expertise lore.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor strategy or persona overlays because the raw source is already strongest on pattern-recognition mechanics.
open_questions: The communication examples are strong, but the intervention layer stays on cue-learning and anomaly-detection transfer.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on cognitive-load-theory, schema-building, and story-based explanation.
antagonists: The source is strongest against confirmation-bias, status-quo-bias, and curse-of-knowledge drift.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against confirmation-bias and status-quo-bias.
